differentials.name: Pleural Effusion (21%) =  earliest asbestos-related pleural abnormality, frequently followed by diffuse pleural thickening + rounded atelectasis
differentials.raw: Pleural Effusion (21%) =  earliest asbestos-related pleural abnormality, frequently followed by diffuse pleural thickening + rounded atelectasis

differentials.name: may be associated with chest pain
differentials.raw: may be associated with chest pain (⅓)

differentials.name: usually small sterile serous / hemorrhagic exudate
differentials.raw: usually small sterile serous / hemorrhagic exudate

differentials.name: Focal Pleural Plaques (65%) =  hyalinized collagen in submesothelial layer of parietal pleura ◊  Most useful marker of asbestos exposure!
differentials.raw: Focal Pleural Plaques (65%) =  hyalinized collagen in submesothelial layer of parietal pleura ◊  Most useful marker of asbestos exposure!

differentials.name: Pleural Calcification (21-25-60%) ◊  HALLMARK of asbestos exposure!
differentials.raw: Pleural Calcification (21-25-60%) ◊  HALLMARK of asbestos exposure!

differentials.name: Diffuse Pleural Thickening
differentials.raw: Diffuse Pleural Thickening (17%) =  smooth uninterrupted diffuse thickening of parietal pleura extending over at least ¼ of chest wall (visceral pleura involved in 90%, but difficult to demonstrate)

differentials.name: may cause restriction of pulmonary function
differentials.raw: may cause restriction of pulmonary function

differentials.name: Appropriate time interval between exposure + detection
differentials.raw: Appropriate time interval between exposure + detection

differentials.name: Radiographic opacities classified as ILO s,t,u
differentials.raw: Radiographic opacities classified as ILO s,t,u

differentials.name: Bilateral crackles at posterior lung bases, NOT cleared by cough
differentials.raw: Bilateral crackles at posterior lung bases, NOT cleared by cough

differentials.name: restrictive pulmonary function tests: progressive reduction of vital capacity + diffusing capacity
differentials.raw: restrictive pulmonary function tests: progressive reduction of vital capacity + diffusing capacity

differentials.name: asbestos bodies in macrophages from bronchoalveolar lavage
differentials.raw: asbestos bodies in macrophages from bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL) fluid (= single asbestos fiber surrounded by segmented protein-iron coat)

context: Prevalence:   3% (higher with increasing levels of asbestos exposure) Latency period:   8-10 years after exposure √  recurrent bilateral effusions √  ± plaque formation DDx:   TB, mesothelioma Frequency:   most common manifestation of exposure; 6% of general population will show plaques; 3-14% of dockyard workers; 58% of insulation workers Latency period:   in 10% (50%) after 20 (40) years Histo:   dense hypocellular undulating collagen fibers often arranged in a "basket weave" pattern ± focal / massive calcifications; may contain large numbers of asbestos fibers (almost exclusively chrysotile
